goal: Fix RR-7: Harden slash-command name parsing in server.py to prevent path traversal, add unit tests, and update documentation.

tasks.name: Harden _expand_slash_command in server.py
tasks.action: In `_expand_slash_command()`, after `name = query[1:].split()[0]`, add validation: strip directory components with `name = os.path.basename(name)` and reject the name if it is empty or still contains `..` (return the original query with a 400-level log warning). This ensures names like `../../etc/passwd` resolve to just `passwd` and cannot escape COMMANDS_DIR. Keep the existing `os.path.isfile` check as a secondary guard.
tasks.verify: run_tests passes. Manually confirm that `os.path.basename` is applied before `os.path.join`.
tasks.done: `_expand_slash_command` strips directory components from the command name before building the file path.

tasks.name: Add unit tests for slash-command path traversal hardening
tasks.action: Import `_expand_slash_command` from `server`. Add a new `TestExpandSlashCommand` class with tests: (1) normal command `/foo` returns file contents (mock `os.path.isfile` + `builtins.open`), (2) traversal attempt `/../../../etc/passwd` is stripped to `passwd.md` lookup and does NOT resolve outside COMMANDS_DIR, (3) `/../../../proc/self/environ` similarly neutralised, (4) non-slash query returned unchanged, (5) unknown command (no matching file) returns original query, (6) command with trailing args `/foo bar baz` extracts only `foo`. Use `tmp_path` or `unittest.mock.patch` for filesystem mocking.
tasks.verify: run_tests passes. All new tests in TestExpandSlashCommand pass.
tasks.done: At least 6 new tests covering normal expansion, traversal attempts, non-slash passthrough, unknown commands, and arg stripping.

tasks.name: Update THREAT_MODEL.md to mark RR-7 as resolved
tasks.action: In §6 Residual Risks, change `RR-7: Slash Command Path Traversal Not Hardened` to strikethrough (~~...~~) with `— RESOLVED` status and date. Update the description to note the fix: `os.path.basename()` strips directory components; traversal is now structurally blocked. In §9 Recommended Mitigations table, change the RR-7 row status from `Open` to `✅ Done (date)`. In §7 STRIDE table, update the claude-server EoP cell to note RR-7 is resolved.
tasks.verify: Grep THREAT_MODEL.md for 'RR-7' — all instances should show resolved/done status. run_tests passes.
tasks.done: RR-7 marked as resolved in all three locations within THREAT_MODEL.md.

tasks.name: Update PLAN.md and CONTEXT.md to reflect RR-7 completion
tasks.action: In PLAN.md §Phase 5 P3 section, change the RR-7 checkbox from `[ ]` to `[X]` and append 'Done (date)' with a brief description. In CONTEXT.md, if slash-command security is mentioned, ensure it reflects the hardened state (basename stripping). No other changes needed.
tasks.verify: Grep PLAN.md for 'RR-7' shows `[X]`. run_tests passes.
tasks.done: RR-7 marked as completed in PLAN.md. CONTEXT.md consistent with the fix.
